metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a targeted AC or extend AC1's grep for `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` specifically to verify the `autolander-gerrit-token` row is absent (e.g. `git grep -n 'autolander-gerrit-token' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing).
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 grep does not include `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` for the term `autolander` (broad term excluded per scope notes), and no other AC verifies that the retired SSM-token row (`/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token`) has actually been removed from the table in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md. The 'What' section names this edit ('drop the retired autolander SSM-token row') but it is unverified by any acceptance criterion.
findings.location: What — grep-driven prose sweep (docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md) / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'AND `git grep -n "autolander-gerrit-token" docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing (the retired SSM row is dropped).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a machine-checkable criterion that verifies the autolander SSM-token row is absent from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md — e.g. grep for the row's known key string (SSM parameter name or table-row label) returning nothing, or grep for 'autolander' in that file returning nothing.
findings.finding: The described deliverable 'drop the retired autolander SSM-token row' from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md has no direct machine-checkable criterion verifying the row is gone. AC1's grep targets specific retired-term strings (autosubmit, lander_down, land-status, etc.), but the SSM-token table row may use descriptive prose (e.g. 'SSM parameter', 'token for the lander service', a row label) that falls outside those patterns — leaving the removal unverified by any AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; What section — 'docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (drop the retired autolander SSM-token row)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: `grep -i 'autolander.*ssm\|ssm.*autolander\|lander.*token\|token.*lander' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing. Alternatively, if the row has a stable label (e.g. a specific parameter name), grep for that label's absence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that positively verifies the autolander SSM-token row has been removed from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (e.g. `grep -c 'autolander\|SSM.*lander\|lander.*token' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns 0, or a line-count/structural check), so the row-drop goal has a faithful observable proof independent of the AC1 term set.
findings.finding:
README.md is listed under 'Rewritten to the new model' in the What section but is absent from AC2's presence check (`for f in CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md`). Wait — re-reading AC2, README.md IS included. Let me re-check…

Actually on re-read AC2 says: `for f in CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md` — README.md IS there. So that is fine.

However, the What section says docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md gets 'drop the retired autolander SSM-token row' but no AC verifies the row was actually dropped — AC1 only checks for absence of specific grep terms, which may not cover the SSM row if the row's text doesn't contain those exact terms (e.g. the row might say 'autolander token' without the exact grep patterns like 'autosubmit', 'lander_down', etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 vs. What section (docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ACs: `grep -iE 'autolander.*token|SSM.*autolander|lander.*ssm' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns nothing — or simply extend the AC1 grep pattern to include `autolander` for this specific file.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `docs/README.md` to the plan's edit-target list (updating or removing its `land-contract.md` / auto-lander entry) OR declare a formal `depends_on: d0e5` so the execution ordering guarantee is visible in the ticket and AC4 is guaranteed to pass.
findings.finding: docs/README.md (the docs index) is an unenumerated consumer of old-model landing prose: line 108 currently reads '— the auto-lander's agent contract: the `land` / `land-status` outcome enum, exit-code table, and JSON output schema…' and links to docs/land-contract.md. The plan's edit-target list omits docs/README.md entirely; no AC in this plan (AC1's grep file-list, AC2's presence check) scans docs/README.md for old-model terms. The plan relies implicitly on sibling d0e5 cleaning this entry, but 5e7a declares no dep on d0e5 — if 5e7a lands before d0e5 the old-model prose in the docs index persists.
findings.location: What — grep-driven prose sweep / Acceptance Criteria (AC1 file list, AC4)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the What section's rewrite list with the edit 'update docs/README.md contributor section: replace the land-contract.md / auto-lander entry with a pointer to the new landing model (or remove it).' Then remove the implicit reliance on d0e5 sequencing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC3's grep to match the literal string `[ADR 0047]` by using `grep -F` or escaping the brackets: `grep -lF 'Superseded by [ADR 0047]' docs/adr/0040-*.md docs/adr/0042-*.md`
findings.finding: AC3's grep pattern `"Superseded by [ADR 0047]"` contains unescaped square brackets, making `[ADR 0047]` a regex character class (matching any single character in {A, D, R, ' ', 0, 4, 7}) rather than the literal string `[ADR 0047]`. The command could silently pass on a file containing only 'Superseded by A' while missing a file that lacks the correct banner, or vice versa — a classic false-positive/false-negative depending on file content.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: `grep -l "Superseded by [ADR 0047]" docs/adr/0040-*.md docs/adr/0042-*.md`
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the AC3 grep with:
```
grep -lF 'Superseded by [ADR 0047]' docs/adr/0040-*.md docs/adr/0042-*.md
```
`-F` (fixed-string mode) disables regex interpretation so `[ADR 0047]` is matched literally. Apply the same fix to the pointer-note check: `grep -lF 'Rebase-If-Necessary' docs/adr/0025-*.md docs/adr/0041-*.md` (the second check is already safe since 'Rebase-If-Necessary' contains no regex metacharacters).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the sweep: update line 108 to describe `land-contract.md` in past-tense / historical terms (or remove the entry), so the docs index no longer advertises the retired auto-lander contract as live guidance.
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` (the docs index, distinct from the top-level `README.md`) contains old-model prose at line 108 — "the auto-lander's agent contract" linking to `land-contract.md` — but `docs/README.md` is NOT in the plan's sweep list and is NOT edited by this story. AC4 (`check_docs_index.py`) only verifies no dead links, not that the prose is updated, so the old-model description of `land-contract.md` survives unaddressed after this story closes.
findings.location: Scope notes / What section (swept files list) + AC4
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/README.md`, change the `land-contract.md` entry from the current description to something like: `- **[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — _Historical:_ the retired auto-lander's outcome enum and exit-code contract (superseded by ADR 0047; landing is now a plain Gerrit Submit once both votes are green).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the sweep (update or remove the `land-contract.md` entry at line 108 that describes it as 'the auto-lander's agent contract') so AC4 (`check_docs_index.py --check`) passes after d0e5 deletes `land-contract.md`.
findings.finding: docs/README.md (the docs index file) is missing from the plan's sweep list and from AC1's grep targets, yet it contains old-model prose at line 108: `**[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — the auto-lander's agent contract: the land / land-status outcome enum, exit-code table, and JSON output schema that an agent acts on to land a change (the design is recorded in ADR-0042)`. Since sibling d0e5 deletes land-contract.md before this plan runs (per ADR 0047 migration sequence), the dead link will cause AC4 (check_docs_index.py --check) to fail — but the plan gives no instruction to fix docs/README.md, so the implementer will hit a failing AC without a corresponding edit target.
findings.location: What section (sweep file list) / AC4 / docs/README.md line 108
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the What section's file list (e.g., 'docs/README.md (remove or update the land-contract.md docs-index entry)') and ensure the entry either points to a replacement doc or is removed entirely, so the docs-index check passes.

findings.finding: AC1's grep pattern includes `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` but the plan explicitly carves out docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md as containing 'fast-forward only' for a *different* concept that is out of scope — so the grep command AS WRITTEN will produce hits in those two files, causing the AC to fail even when all required work is correctly done. The allowed-hits whitelist in the AC prose does not include those two files, creating an irreconcilable conflict between the command and its stated pass condition.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add `--exclude-dir` / pathspec negation to the grep, for example:
```
git grep -icE 'autosubmit|auto-?lander|land-contract|land-status|lander_down|fast[- ]forward[- ]only|\bFFO\b|rebase[- ]on[- ]behalf|land <change>|--wait|both votes green' \
  -- '*.md' \
  ':(exclude)docs/adr/0010*.md' \
  ':(exclude)docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'
```
Then the whitelist in the prose (only 0040, 0042, 0047, 0025, 0041, and hyperlinks) matches what the command can actually produce.

findings.finding: AC1's grep pattern includes `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` and says hits are allowed ONLY in the two superseded ADRs and a narrow allowlist — but Scope Notes separately state that 'GitHub-mirror "fast-forward only" in docs/adr/0010 + docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md is a different concept and out of scope.' Those two files are NOT in AC1's allowlist, so an implementer running AC1 verbatim will get unexpected hits from those files and incorrectly conclude the criterion fails.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 (old-model ABSENCE grep) vs. Scope Notes paragraph 3
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC1 allowlist parenthetical to read: '…and the ADR-0047 filename inside hyperlinks. (Also allowed: `fast[- ]forward[- ]only` hits in docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, which refer to the unrelated GitHub push setting — see Scope Notes.)' Or split the grep into two commands: one for the autolander-specific terms (no fast-forward) and a separate one for the FFO/fast-forward terms with a broader allowlist.

findings.finding: The 'What' section lists docs/adr/0025 and docs/adr/0041 as deliverables ('Light pointer-note … prior-model logic left for a separate review'), but no AC verifies that the pointer notes were actually added to those files — AC1 only checks that old terms are absent, not that the new pointer note is present.
findings.location: What section ('Banner-only / light pointer-note' paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC3b: `grep -l 'Rebase-If-Necessary' docs/adr/0025-*.md docs/adr/0041-*.md` must list both files.

findings.finding: The AC-1 grep allowlist does not include docs/adr/0010 and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, yet the plan's own 'Scope notes' parenthetical acknowledges those files contain 'fast-forward only' (a different concept, explicitly out of scope). The AC as written will spuriously fail after a correct implementation because those files are not carved out of the grep pattern or the expected-survivors list.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet (Old-model ABSENCE grep AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC-1 grep with: `git grep -icE "autosubmit|auto-?lander|land-contract|land-status|lander_down|fast[- ]forward[- ]only|\bFFO\b|rebase[- ]on[- ]behalf|land <change>|--wait|both votes green" -- '*.md' ':!docs/adr/0010-*.md' ':!docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md'` and update the expected-survivors list to document why those two files are excluded.

findings.finding: AC1's pass condition (old-model grep hits appear ONLY in the two superseded ADRs, ADR 0047, and pointer-note ADRs) will not be satisfied as long as docs/land-contract.md remains in the repo, because that file contains multiple AC1-triggering terms (lander_down, Autosubmit, land-status, 'land <change>', FFO). The plan explicitly defers docs/land-contract.md deletion to sibling story d0e5 and does not name d0e5 as a prerequisite or add docs/land-contract.md to the AC1 allowlist — creating a contradiction: AC1 requires old-model terms absent from non-exempted files, but a live non-exempted file (docs/land-contract.md) will still contain them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; Scope notes ('infra/ autolander references are sibling bcf0's scope')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope notes: 'docs/land-contract.md still contains old-model terms (lander_down, Autosubmit, land-status, land <change>); this story does NOT delete it (that is d0e5). AC1 therefore requires d0e5 to complete first, OR add docs/land-contract.md to the AC1 exemption list alongside the superseded ADRs.' Then update AC1's allowlist to include docs/land-contract.md with the qualifier 'pending deletion by d0e5'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
